protocol: Seventh Law clause 5 — Recurring Bluff Hunt (Go-side)
samples_method: operator-selected from lava-api-go: utils_test.go, comments_test.go, passthrough_test.go (auth), topic_test.go (handlers), search_test.go (rutracker)

targets.production_under_test: rutracker.getTitle (utils.go) — strips [tag] runs from rutracker forum/search row titles before they reach the user
targets.mutation_applied: In utils.go getTitle, replaced the regex-strip + bracket-cleanup body with `return titleWithTags` (skip the strip-tags logic entirely).
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: TestGetTitle_StripsTags: utils_test.go:35 getTitle("[Genre] Title [1080p]"): got "[Genre] Title [1080p]", want "Title"; getTitle("[Tag] Single"): got "[Tag] Single", want "Single"
targets.remediation: N/A — primary assertion is on the user-visible title string the row would render. Falsifiable on the strip-tags branch; the test covers each transformation row of the table.

targets.production_under_test: rutracker.ParseCommentsPage (comments.go) — drops the magnet-bearing OP from the comments list so a comments rendering does not double-count the torrent body
targets.mutation_applied: In comments.go ParseCommentsPage, commented out the entire firstPost magnet-removal block so the magnet-bearing OP leaks into the post list.
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: TestParseCommentsPage_HappyPath: comments_test.go:66 expected 2 posts (magnet-bearing first post removed), got 3; TestParseCommentsPage_DropsMagnetFirstPost: comments_test.go:102 expected 2 posts (magnet-bearing first post MUST be dropped), got 3
targets.remediation: N/A — the dedicated DropsMagnetFirstPost test pins the magnet-removal contract end-to-end; it failed loudly under the mutation. HappyPath also asserts the count.

targets.production_under_test: auth.UpstreamCookie (passthrough.go) — translates the Auth-Token header into the upstream Cookie value; SP-3.5 bug guard against double-prefixing 'bb_session=' on a token that already contains '='
targets.mutation_applied: In passthrough.go UpstreamCookie, removed the `if strings.Contains(tok, "=") { return tok }` branch so the function unconditionally prepends `bb_session=` (re-introducing the historical SP-3.5 bug).
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: TestUpstreamCookieForwardsCookieLineVerbatim: passthrough_test.go:119 got="bb_session=bb_session=0-47500467-..." want="bb_session=0-47500467-..." (cookie line MUST be forwarded verbatim per SP-3.5; double-prefixing breaks search/favorites/etc.); TestUpstreamCookie_TokenWithEqualsForwardsVerbatim: passthrough_test.go:133 got="bb_session=session_v2=opaque-token-value" want "session_v2=opaque-token-value"
targets.remediation: N/A — this is the dedicated regression guard for the historical bug; the test commit body in the file already documents the falsifiability rehearsal. Primary assertion is on the bytes the upstream Cookie header would carry.

targets.production_under_test: handlers.TopicHandler.GetTopic (topic.go) — GET /topic/{id} cache-hit short-circuit and realm-keyed cache slot
targets.mutation_applied: In topic.go GetTopic, wrapped the cache-hit short-circuit in `if false { ... }` so the handler always falls through to the scraper (cache lookup never returns the cached body).
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: TestTopicHandler_GetTopic_HappyPath_HitsScraperOnFirstCall_HitsCacheOnSecond: topic_test.go:98 /topic/{id} scraper calls=2 want 1 (cache should have served the second request); TestTopicHandler_GetTopic_AuthRealmHashAffectsCacheKey: topic_test.go:259 auth-2: scraper calls=3 want 2 (realm-keyed entry should have served the second hit)
targets.remediation: N/A — request dispatched through real Gin engine + real auth.GinMiddleware via httptest. Primary failure signal is on observable scraper-call count which translates to upstream HTTP load — a user-visible-via-cost metric that the cache layer is supposed to suppress. Body bytes are also asserted on the response (Content-Type + JSON match) so it is not a count-only verification.

targets.production_under_test: rutracker.searchSortTypeValue + GetSearchPage upstream URL builder (search.go) — maps SearchSortTypeDto to rutracker `o=` query value, including Size→7
targets.mutation_applied: In search.go searchSortTypeValue, replaced the Size case body `return "7"` with `return "10"` (collide with the Seeds value).
targets.remediation: N/A — the test asserts on the actual upstream-URL query bytes captured at the httptest server, which is the surface rutracker.org observes. Falsifiable on every parameter mapping (Categories→f, Author→pn, AuthorID→pid, SortType→o, SortOrder→s, Period→tm, Page→start) by the same harness.

summary.verdict: All 5 operator-selected Go-side tests survived the mutation-and-revert protocol. Each test failed cleanly with a clear assertion message naming the broken behavior, and passed again after revert. No bluff fakes detected at this phase boundary on the Go-side. The Sixth Law clause-3 'primary assertion on user-visible state' is satisfied by every target: getTitle returns the rendered title; ParseCommentsPage emits the post array a comments view would render; UpstreamCookie produces the upstream Cookie bytes; GetTopic emits HTTP body and Content-Type plus an observable scraper-call cost; GetSearchPage emits the upstream URL bytes.
summary.post_revert_verification_run: cd lava-api-go && go test ./internal/rutracker/... ./internal/auth/... ./internal/handlers/... -> ok (cached after first uncached run, all green)
